items.title: Is It Really a Good Sign When Executives Buy Their Own Stock? We Ran the Numbers
items.description: We looked at 1,400 insider purchases over the past five years to find out whether they give share prices a boost.

items.title: Analysts flag alarming correlation amid tech sell-off
items.description: The tech sell-off continued on Wall Street as the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite and the S&P 500 benchmark sank on Feb. 4. While the former was down 1.8%, the latter was down 0.6% at press time. Nearly all the Magnificent Seven stocks were trading in the red zone at the time of writing. ...

items.title: Dow Jones Futures Rise; Google Beats Views, Reports Huge AI Spending
items.description: The rotation out of techs continued in a divided stock market, but Eli Lilly jumped. Google rose late on earnings and huge capital spending plans.

items.title: Alphabet won’t talk about the Google-Apple AI deal, even to investors
items.description: Alphabet CEO skipped an analyst's question about Apple on the company's earnings call.

items.title: Google parent earnings beat projections amid plans to invest deeply in AI
items.description: Alphabet reports $34.5bn profit and revenue soars 48% in recent quarter as it plans a sharp increase in AI spending
